hypothesis: If Bayesian optimization guided by polymer-specific molecular representations is applied to a fragment-generated polyamine library with adsorption capacities estimated by a two-stage MD+GCMC workflow, then it will identify higher-CO2-uptake candidates within a fixed small simulation budget than BO with general-purpose representations or random sampling because domain-tuned embeddings yield more accurate surrogates and more sample-efficient exploration even from low-performing initializations.
